words.definitionEn: the empty space at the side of a written or printed page
words.exampleEn: the left-hand/right-hand margin

words.definitionEn: an object or a sign that shows the position of something
words.exampleEn: a boundary marker

words.definitionEn: affecting or involving a large number of people or things
words.exampleEn: The world faces the tremendous problem of mass unemployment.

words.definitionEn: a large amount of a substance that does not have a definite shape or form
words.exampleEn: She saw a large dark mass in the water.

words.definitionEn: very large, heavy and solid
words.exampleEn: a massive rock

words.definitionEn: a man who has people working for him, often as servants in his home
words.exampleEn: They lived in fear of their master.

words.definitionEn: to learn or understand something completely
words.exampleEn: She never completely mastered the art of lip-reading.

words.definitionEn: having the same colour, pattern, style, etc. and therefore looking attractive together
words.exampleEn: The two sisters wore matching outfits.

words.definitionEn: a friend
words.exampleEn: They've been best mates since school.

words.definitionEn: to have sex in order to produce young
words.exampleEn: Do foxes ever mate with dogs?

words.definitionEn: connected with money, possessions, etc. rather than with the needs of the mind or spirit
words.exampleEn: material possessions/wealth

words.definitionEn: made or produced to copy something natural; not real
words.exampleEn: an artificial limb/flower/sweetener/fertilizer

words.definitionEn: as large, fast, etc. as is possible, or the most that is possible or allowed
words.exampleEn: the maximum amount/number of something

words.definitionEn: the greatest amount, size, speed, etc. that is possible, recorded or allowed
words.exampleEn: a maximum of 30 children in a class

words.definitionEn: the head of the government of a town or city, etc., elected by the public
words.exampleEn: the Mayor of New York

words.definitionEn: an action, an object or a system by which a result is achieved; a way of achieving or doing something
words.exampleEn: means of something, Email is a highly effective means of communication.

words.definitionEn: connected with art or artists
words.exampleEn: the artistic works of the period

words.definitionEn: the act or the process of finding the size, quantity or degree of something
words.exampleEn: the metric system of measurement

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is repairing machines, especially the engines of vehicles
words.exampleEn: a car/motor mechanic

words.definitionEn: operated by power from an engine
words.exampleEn: a mechanical device/toy/clock

words.definitionEn: a set of moving parts in a machine that performs a task
words.exampleEn: a delicate watch mechanism

words.definitionEn: a flat piece of metal, usually like a coin in shape, that is given to the winner of a competition or to somebody who has been brave, for example in war
words.exampleEn: to win a gold medal in the Olympics

words.definitionEn: pictures and photographs prepared for books, magazines, etc.
words.exampleEn: Can you let me have the finished artwork for the poster by Friday?

words.definitionEn: a drug or another form of medicine that you take to prevent or to treat an illness; treatment involving drugs
words.exampleEn: Are you currently taking any medication?

words.definitionEn: a way of communicating information, etc. to people
words.exampleEn: the medium of radio/television

words.definitionEn: to become or make something become liquid as a result of heating
words.exampleEn: The snow showed no sign of melting.

words.definitionEn: the state of being a member of a group, a club, an organization, etc.
words.exampleEn: membership of something, (British English), Who is eligible to apply for membership of the association?

words.definitionEn: worth remembering or easy to remember, especially because of being special or unusual
words.exampleEn: The holiday provided many memorable moments.

words.definitionEn: a word or phrase used to describe somebody/something else, in a way that is different from its normal use, in order to show that the two things have the same qualities and to make the description more powerful, for example She has a heart of stone; the use of such words and phrases
words.exampleEn: a game of football used as a metaphor for the competitive struggle of life

words.definitionEn: by chance; in a way that was not planned
words.exampleEn: As I turned around, I accidentally hit him in the face.

words.definitionEn: connected with soldiers or the armed forces
words.exampleEn: We may have to take military action.

words.definitionEn: soldiers; the armed forces
words.exampleEn: The military was/were called in to deal with the riot.

words.definitionEn: feeling shame or feeling embarrassed about somebody/something or because of something you have done
words.exampleEn: ashamed of something, She was deeply ashamed of her behaviour at the party.

words.definitionEn: a person who works in a mine taking out coal, gold, diamonds, etc.
words.exampleEn: Rescuers are trying to save miners trapped underground after a gas explosion.

words.definitionEn: a substance that is naturally present in the earth and is not formed from animal or vegetable matter, for example gold and salt. Some minerals are also present in food and drink and in the human body and are essential for good health.
words.exampleEn: a country rich in mineral resources

words.definitionEn: to one side; out of the way
words.exampleEn: She pulled the curtain aside.

words.definitionEn: the smallest that is possible or allowed; extremely small
words.exampleEn: a minimum charge/price

words.definitionEn: the smallest or lowest amount that is possible, required or recorded
words.exampleEn: The class needs a minimum of six students to continue.

words.definitionEn: a senior member of the government who is in charge of a government department or a branch of one
words.exampleEn: the Minister of Education

words.definitionEn: not very large, important or serious
words.exampleEn: The new plan involves widening a minor road through the valley.

words.definitionEn: the smaller part of a group; less than half of the people or things in a large group
words.exampleEn: Only a small minority of students is/are interested in politics these days.

words.definitionEn: very unhappy or uncomfortable
words.exampleEn: We were cold, wet and thoroughly miserable.

words.definitionEn: that cannot exist or be done; not possible
words.exampleEn: almost/virtually/nearly impossible

words.definitionEn: to not understand or judge somebody/something correctly
words.exampleEn: mistake somebody/something, I admit that I mistook his intentions.

words.definitionEn: consisting of different qualities or elements
words.exampleEn: a mixed diet

words.definitionEn: a particular part or feature of a situation, an idea, a problem, etc.; a way in which it may be considered
words.exampleEn: aspect of something, The book aims to cover all aspects of city life.

words.definitionEn: a particular way of doing something; a particular type of something
words.exampleEn: a mode of communication

words.definitionEn: to create a copy or description of an activity, a situation, etc. so that you can study it before dealing with the real thing
words.exampleEn: The program can model a typical home page for you.

words.definitionEn: not very large, expensive, important, etc.
words.exampleEn: modest improvements/reforms

words.definitionEn: to change something slightly, especially in order to make it more suitable for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: Patients are taught how to modify their diet.

words.definitionEn: a screen that shows information from a computer; a television screen used to show particular kinds of information
words.exampleEn: The details of today's flights are displayed on the monitor.

words.definitionEn: to watch and check something over a period of time in order to see how it develops, so that you can make any necessary changes
words.exampleEn: monitor something, Each student's progress is closely monitored.

words.definitionEn: (in stories) an imaginary creature that is very large, ugly and frightening
words.exampleEn: a monster with three heads

words.definitionEn: happening once a month or every month
words.exampleEn: a monthly meeting/visit/magazine

words.definitionEn: a building, column, statue, etc. built to remind people of a famous person or event
words.exampleEn: A monument to him was erected in St Paul's Cathedral.

words.definitionEn: connected with principles of right and wrong behaviour
words.exampleEn: a moral issue/dilemma

words.definitionEn: standards or principles of good behaviour, especially in matters of sexual relationships
words.exampleEn: Young people these days have no morals.

words.definitionEn: used to introduce some new information that adds to or supports what you have said previously
words.exampleEn: A talented artist, he was, moreover, a writer of some note.

words.definitionEn: a legal agreement by which a bank or similar organization lends you money to buy a house, etc., and you pay the money back over a particular number of years; the sum of money that you borrow
words.exampleEn: to apply for/take out/pay off a mortgage

words.definitionEn: a building where Muslims go to worship (= praise God)
words.exampleEn: I used to pray at the local mosque with my father and grandfather.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of moving or the way something moves
words.exampleEn: What was Newton's first law of motion?

words.definitionEn: to be the reason why somebody does something or behaves in a particular way
words.exampleEn: motivate somebody (to do something), What motivates people to carry out such attacks?

words.definitionEn: the reason why somebody does something or behaves in a particular way
words.exampleEn: motivation (behind something), What is the motivation behind this sudden change?

words.definitionEn: having an engine; using the power of an engine
words.exampleEn: The street is closed to motor vehicles.

words.definitionEn: a device that uses electricity, petrol, etc. to produce movement and makes a machine, a vehicle, a boat, etc. work
words.exampleEn: An electric motor is used to pump the water.

words.definitionEn: to organize and begin something
words.exampleEn: Residents mounted a campaign to fight the plans.

words.definitionEn: to provide somebody with a room or place to sleep, live or sit
words.exampleEn: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.

words.definitionEn: causing strong, often sad, feelings about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a deeply moving experience

words.definitionEn: many in number; involving many different people or things
words.exampleEn: The shape appears multiple times within each painting.

words.definitionEn: to add a number to itself a particular number of times
words.exampleEn: The children are already learning to multiply and divide.

words.definitionEn: difficult to understand or explain; strange
words.exampleEn: He died in mysterious circumstances.

words.definitionEn: a story from ancient times, especially one that was told to explain natural events or to describe the early history of a people; this type of story
words.exampleEn: ancient Greek myths

words.definitionEn: not wearing any clothes
words.exampleEn: She was clutching the sheet around her naked body.

words.definitionEn: to become or make something less wide
words.exampleEn: This is where the river narrows.

words.definitionEn: very bad or unpleasant
words.exampleEn: He had a nasty accident.

words.definitionEn: a citizen of a particular country
words.exampleEn: Polish nationals living in Germany

words.definitionEn: the skill or the process of planning a route for a ship or other vehicle and taking it there
words.exampleEn: a maker of in-car navigation systems

words.definitionEn: to make a judgement about the nature or quality of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: assess somebody/something, Accurately assessing environmental impacts is very complex.

words.definitionEn: near in position; not far away
words.exampleEn: Her mother lived in a nearby town.

words.definitionEn: a short distance from somebody/something; not far away
words.exampleEn: They live nearby.

words.definitionEn: tidy and in order; carefully done or arranged
words.exampleEn: She kept her desk extremely neat.

words.definitionEn: the fact that something must happen or be done; the need for something
words.exampleEn: necessity for something, We recognize the necessity for a written agreement.

words.definitionEn: an opinion or a judgement about somebody/something that has been thought about very carefully
words.exampleEn: a detailed assessment of the risks involved

words.definitionEn: a word or statement that means ‘no’; an act of refusing to do something or of denying something
words.exampleEn: in the negative, She answered in the negative (= said ‘no’).

words.definitionEn: to try to reach an agreement by formal discussion
words.exampleEn: negotiate (with somebody) (for something), The government will not negotiate with terrorists.

words.definitionEn: formal discussion between people who are trying to reach an agreement
words.exampleEn: peace/trade/contract negotiations

words.definitionEn: a person or thing that is valuable or useful to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: In his job, patience is an invaluable asset.

words.definitionEn: any of the long fibres that carry messages between the brain and parts of the body, enabling you to move, feel pain, etc.
words.exampleEn: the optic nerve

words.definitionEn: to give somebody something that they can use, or some work or responsibility
words.exampleEn: assign something (to somebody), The teacher assigned a different task to each of the children.

words.definitionEn: not supporting or helping either side in a disagreement, competition, etc.
words.exampleEn: Journalists are supposed to be politically neutral.

words.definitionEn: despite something that you have just mentioned
words.exampleEn: There is little chance that we will succeed in changing the law. Nevertheless, it is important that we try.

words.definitionEn: recently
words.exampleEn: a newly qualified doctor

words.definitionEn: a dream that is very frightening or unpleasant
words.exampleEn: He still has nightmares about the accident.

words.definitionEn: help or support
words.exampleEn: technical/economic/military assistance

words.definitionEn: a situation or a pattern of behaviour that is usual or expected
words.exampleEn: The new design is a departure from the norm.

words.definitionEn: a small book of plain paper for writing notes in
words.exampleEn: The police officer wrote the details down in his notebook.

words.definitionEn: an idea, a belief or an understanding of something
words.exampleEn: notion of something, a political system based on the notions of equality and liberty

words.definitionEn: to make a connection between people or things in your mind
words.exampleEn: associate somebody/something with somebody/something, I always associate the smell of baking with my childhood.

words.definitionEn: a person who writes novels
words.exampleEn: a romantic/historical novelist

words.definitionEn: at the present time, in contrast with the past
words.exampleEn: Nowadays most kids prefer going online to reading books.

words.definitionEn: if one thing is associated with another, the two things are connected because they happen together or one thing causes the other
words.exampleEn: associated with doing something, the risks associated with taking drugs

words.definitionEn: existing in large numbers
words.exampleEn: He has been late on numerous occasions.

words.definitionEn: the job or skill of caring for people who are sick or injured
words.exampleEn: a career in nursing

words.definitionEn: the process by which living things receive the food necessary for them to grow and be healthy
words.exampleEn: advice on diet and nutrition

words.definitionEn: the quality or fact of being very fat, in a way that is not healthy
words.exampleEn: Obesity can increase the risk of heart disease.

words.definitionEn: to do what you are told or expected to do
words.exampleEn: obey something, to obey a command/an order/rules/the law

words.definitionEn: an official group of people who have joined together for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: Do you belong to any professional or trade associations?

words.definitionEn: to say that you disagree with or oppose something
words.exampleEn: If nobody objects, we'll postpone the meeting till next week.

words.definitionEn: not influenced by personal feelings or opinions; considering only facts
words.exampleEn: an objective assessment of the situation

words.definitionEn: something that you are trying to achieve
words.exampleEn: the primary/principal/key objective

words.definitionEn: the state of being forced to do something because it is your duty, or because of a law, etc.
words.exampleEn: obligation to do something, You are under no obligation to buy anything.

words.definitionEn: the act of watching somebody/something carefully for a period of time, especially to learn something
words.exampleEn: Most information was collected by direct observation of the animals' behaviour.

words.definitionEn: to see or notice somebody/something
words.exampleEn: observe somebody/something, Have you observed any changes lately?

words.definitionEn: a person who watches somebody/something
words.exampleEn: According to observers, the plane exploded shortly after take-off.

words.definitionEn: to think or accept that something is true but without having proof of it
words.exampleEn: assume (that)…, It is reasonable to assume (that) the economy will continue to improve.

words.definitionEn: a situation, an event, etc. that makes it difficult for you to do or achieve something
words.exampleEn: So far, we have managed to overcome all the obstacles that have been placed in our path.

words.definitionEn: to get something, especially by making an effort
words.exampleEn: to obtain information/data/results

words.definitionEn: sometimes but not often
words.exampleEn: We occasionally meet for a drink after work.

words.definitionEn: a job or profession
words.exampleEn: Please state your name, age and occupation below.

words.definitionEn: a belief or feeling that something is true or that something will happen, although there is no proof
words.exampleEn: an underlying/implicit assumption

words.definitionEn: to fill or use a space, an area or an amount of time
words.exampleEn: The bed seemed to occupy most of the room.

words.definitionEn: an illegal act
words.exampleEn: a criminal/serious/minor/sexual offence

words.definitionEn: to make somebody feel upset because of something you say or do that is rude or embarrassing
words.exampleEn: They'll be offended if you don't go to their wedding.

words.definitionEn: a person who commits a crime
words.exampleEn: a persistent/serious/violent, etc. offender

words.definitionEn: rude in a way that causes somebody to feel upset or annoyed because it shows a lack of respect
words.exampleEn: offensive remarks

words.definitionEn: to travel or go somewhere with somebody/something
words.exampleEn: accompany somebody/something + adv./prep., His wife accompanied him on the trip.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody that something is definitely true or is definitely going to happen, especially when they have doubts about it
words.exampleEn: assure somebody (that)…, You think I did it deliberately, but I assure you (that) I did not.

words.definitionEn: a person who is in a position of authority in a large organization
words.exampleEn: She is the government official in charge of the project.

words.definitionEn: very surprising; difficult to believe
words.exampleEn: She ran 100m in an astonishing 10.6 seconds.

words.definitionEn: continuing to exist or develop
words.exampleEn: an ongoing debate/discussion/process

words.definitionEn: a space or hole that somebody/something can pass through
words.exampleEn: We could see the stars through an opening in the roof.

words.definitionEn: without hiding any feelings, opinions or information
words.exampleEn: Can you talk openly about sex with your parents?

words.definitionEn: a dramatic work in which all or most of the words are sung to music; works of this type as an art form or entertainment
words.exampleEn: Puccini’s operas

words.definitionEn: to work in a particular way
words.exampleEn: Most domestic freezers operate at below −18°C.
